title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe - Easy & Authentic
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy recipe. Creamy, rich, and bursting with Indian flavors, this dish is perfect for lunch or dinner.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of creamy, rich, and authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the velvety richness of homemade Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani), a true Indian classic.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a beloved Indian classic known for its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce and tender pieces of marinated chicken. This iconic dish offers a perfect balance of subtle sweetness, tang, and mild spice, making it a comforting and incredibly flavorful meal. It pairs wonderfully with naan bread or basmati rice for an unforgettable culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, lemon juice, 1/2 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary, to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove the chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt the remaining 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and saute until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, green cardamom pods, bay leaf, and cinnamon stick. Cook for another 2 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer and Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato puree, remaining 1/2 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder, and 1/2 tsp garam masala. Add the soaked cashews and 1/2 cup water. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-12 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Remove from heat, discard the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods (if desired, or blend them). Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan for an extra smooth texture.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Sauce
instructions.text: Return the pureed sauce to the pan and bring it to a gentle simmer. Add the cooked chicken pieces to the sauce. Stir in the sugar and crushed fenugreek leaves (Kasuri Methi). Cook for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the flavors of the sauce.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the heavy cream and an extra knob of butter (if desired for richness). Heat through gently for 1-2 minutes, but do not boil after adding cream to prevent curdling. Taste and adjust salt and sugar if needed. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with naan bread, roti, or basmati rice.

tips: For an extra rich and smoky flavor, char the chicken slightly in a tandoor or under a broiler after marinating, before adding to the sauce.
Soaking cashews is crucial for a smooth, creamy sauce without any gritty texture. You can also substitute cashews with blanched almonds for a similar effect.
Always add cream at the very end and avoid vigorous boiling to prevent it from curdling, ensuring your Butter Chicken remains velvety smooth.
article:
Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) stands as a crown jewel in the vast and vibrant repertoire of Indian cuisine. Renowned globally for its rich, creamy texture and subtly spiced tomato-based gravy, it’s a dish that evokes comfort, indulgence, and exquisite flavor. Perfect for a hearty Indian lunch, Butter Chicken is not just a meal; it's an experience, a culinary journey that transports your taste buds straight to the bustling streets of Delhi or a warm Indian home. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a curious beginner, mastering this iconic dish is incredibly rewarding, promising a delicious feast that will impress family and friends alike.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

At its core, Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani as it's traditionally known, is a dish consisting of tender pieces of chicken cooked in a mildly spiced, creamy tomato sauce. The name "Murgh Makhani" literally translates to "chicken with butter," hinting at the generous use of butter and cream that gives the gravy its signature richness and silky smooth texture. Originating in Delhi, India, in the 1950s, it's believed to have been created by Kundan Lal Gujral at the Moti Mahal restaurant, using leftover tandoori chicken in a tomato and butter gravy. This accidental innovation quickly became a sensation, cementing its place as one of the most beloved Indian dishes worldwide. Its distinct flavor profile balances the tanginess of tomatoes with the sweetness of cream and a medley of aromatic spices like garam masala, fenugreek, and cardamom, making it irresistibly delicious.

## Ingredients for Authentic Butter Chicken

Creating an authentic Butter Chicken requires a few key components, each playing a vital role in building its complex flavor.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri for color)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   Salt to taste

For the Makhani (Butter) Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 teaspoon dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi), crushed
*   800g canned crushed tomatoes or 6-7 ripe fresh tomatoes, pureed
*   1/2 cup cashew paste (made by soaking 1/4 cup cashews in warm water for 30 mins, then blending)
*   1/2 cup heavy cream
*   1 tablespoon sugar (adjust to taste)
*   Salt to taste
*   Fresh coriander leaves for garnish

## Step-by-Step Butter Chicken Recipe

Follow these steps to create a show-stopping Butter Chicken that tastes just like your favorite Indian restaurant.

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, to allow the flavors to penetrate.

2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Heat a grill pan, heavy-bottomed skillet, or oven to high heat. Cook the marinated chicken in batches until slightly charred and cooked through. If using an oven, place chicken on a baking sheet and bake at 200°C (400°F) for 15-20 minutes. Set aside.

3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt butter and oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 8-10 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

4.  **Add Spices and Tomatoes:** Stir in the red chili powder, turmeric, ground coriander, and garam masala. Cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly, to bloom the spices. Immediately add the crushed tomatoes (or fresh tomato puree). Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the oil separates. This step is crucial for developing the tomato's deep flavor.

5.  **Achieve Creaminess:** Stir in the crushed dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) and the cashew paste. Cook for another 5 minutes, stirring well. The cashews will add richness and help thicken the sauce.

6.  **Blend (Optional but Recommended):** For an extra smooth sauce, carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. Strain the sauce back into the pot through a fine-mesh sieve if you desire an ultra-silky texture, pressing down on solids.

7.  **Combine and Finish:** Return the smooth sauce to the pot. Stir in the cooked chicken pieces. Add the heavy cream and sugar. Mix well and let it simmer gently for 5-10 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the sauce. Taste and adjust salt and sugar as needed.

8.  **Garnish and Serve:** Garnish with fresh coriander leaves. Your homemade Butter Chicken is ready!

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

Achieving restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is entirely possible with a few expert tricks:

*   **Quality Ingredients:** Use ripe, good-quality tomatoes for the sauce. San Marzano canned tomatoes work wonderfully if fresh ones aren't in season. High-quality butter and fresh cream also make a significant difference.
*   **Marination Time:** Don't skimp on marination. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Charring the Chicken:** Giving the chicken a slight char before adding it to the sauce adds a smoky depth (tandoori flavor) that elevates the dish. A grill pan or broiler works best.
*   **Slow Simmering the Sauce:** Allow the tomato sauce to simmer slowly and thoroughly. This reduces the acidity of the tomatoes and concentrates their flavor, leading to a richer, sweeter gravy.
*   **Cashew Paste:** Don't skip the cashew paste; it's essential for the creamy texture and subtle sweetness of the Murgh Makhani. Soak cashews in hot water for at least 30 minutes before blending for a smoother paste.
*   **Kasoori Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi) are non-negotiable. Crush them between your palms before adding to release their aromatic, slightly bitter, and sweet notes that are characteristic of authentic Butter Chicken.
*   **Adjust Sweetness/Acidity:** Depending on your tomatoes, you might need to adjust the sugar. A pinch of sugar balances the acidity and enhances the overall flavor.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of Indian breads and rice. Here are some classic serving suggestions:

*   **Naan Bread:** Soft, fluffy naan (plain, garlic, or butter naan) is the ultimate accompaniment, perfect for scooping up every last bit of that rich gravy.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or a fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a lovely contrast, soaking up the sauce beautifully.
*   **Raita:** A cooling cucumber raita (yogurt dip) can offer a refreshing counterpoint to the richness of the Murgh Makhani.
*   **Salad:** A simple side salad of sliced onions, cucumbers, and tomatoes with a squeeze of lemon juice adds freshness.
*   **Papadums:** Crispy papadums offer a delightful crunch and can be used as an edible scoop.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is perfect, there are many ways to adapt it to dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Version:** Replace the chicken with paneer (Indian cheese), tofu, or mixed vegetables (like cauliflower and peas) for a delicious vegetarian Murgh Makhani.
*   **Vegan Version:** Use plant-based yogurt for marination, coconut oil instead of butter, and full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream instead of dairy cream. Ensure your other ingredients are also vegan-friendly.
*   **Spicier Butter Chicken:** For those who prefer more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the sauce.
*   **Smoked Flavor:** If you don't have a grill, you can impart a smoky flavor by placing a small, hot piece of charcoal in a small bowl within your finished sauce, drizzling a teaspoon of ghee over it, and quickly covering the pot for a few minutes.
*   **Healthier Option:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream, opting for a larger proportion of cashew paste for creaminess and using low-fat yogurt in the marinade.

Making Butter Chicken at home is a rewarding culinary adventure that brings the flavors of India right to your dining table. This beloved Indian lunch recipe, with its tender chicken and luxurious, creamy sauce, is a testament to the magic of Indian spices and cooking techniques. Follow this guide, infuse it with your passion, and prepare to savor one of the world's most cherished dishes.
